content.problemStatement:
You are given a `2D` matrix of size n×mn×mn×m consisting only of 0′s0's0′s and 1′s1's1′s. Your task is to determine the index of the row that contains the maximum number of 1′s1's1′s. In the case of multiple rows having the same maximum count of 1′s1's1′s, return the index of the first such row.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains two integers nnn and mmm, representing the number of rows and columns in the matrix,
respectively.
The next nnn lines each contain mmm integers (000 or 111), representing the elements of the matrix.




Output Format

For each test case, output a single integer, the index (000-based) of the row that has the maximum number of 1′s1's1′s. If all rows contain only 0′s0's0′s, output −1-1−1.
constraints: The elements of the matrix are either 000 or 111.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: The second row (index 111) contains two 1′s1's1′s, which is the maximum count of 1′s1's1′s among all rows.
Test Case 2: The fourth row (index 333) contains four 1′s1's1′s, which is the maximum count of 1′s1's1′s among all rows.
Test Case 3: Only 0′s0's0′s are present.
